facts.text: Lockport’s largest employer, General Motors Components, began locally in 1912 as the Harrison Radiator Company before becoming a GM division.
State grants aim to rebuild Lockport’s historic “Flight of Five” locks into a living‑history site complete with boat rides and reenactors.
The Lockport Erie Canal Museum occupies an old lock‑control structure between modern Locks 34 and 35 and the original “Flight of Five” spillway.
The Niagara County Historical Society complex at 215 Niagara Street houses exhibits that chronicle the county’s rich history.
Lockport was originally built around a double set of five smaller combined locks that powered the Erie Canal.
Local officials and businesses are promoting Lockport’s canal heritage as a regional attraction that could draw thousands of tourists each year.
